science.name: Science / Nature
science.analogyDomain: Kitchen, garden, body, weather — things the child can see, touch, or feel
science.howItWorks: Input → transformation → output. Always explain the MECHANISM, not just the name.
science.exampleHook: Use everyday observations: ice melting, leaves turning yellow, breathing faster after running.

history.name: History
history.analogyDomain: Human story with real people making real choices under real pressure
history.howItWorks: Root cause → turning point → outcome. Lead with PEOPLE, not abstract forces.
history.exampleHook: One specific moment or person that shows the big event in miniature.

geography.name: Geography
geography.analogyDomain: Map, journey, physical world — where things are and WHY they are there
geography.howItWorks: Physical feature → human consequence. Always use compass directions (N/S/E/W).
geography.exampleHook: Connect rivers/mountains/coasts to how people farm, trade, or build cities there.

english.name: English / Grammar
english.analogyDomain: Building blocks, recipe — parts fitting together to make meaning
english.howItWorks: Show the WRONG version first (the confusion), then the rule that fixes it.
english.exampleHook: Use a pair: bad sentence → good sentence. Let the contrast teach.

technology.name: Technology / Computers
technology.analogyDomain: Postal system, recipe, brain — physical-world analogies for digital concepts
technology.howItWorks: Problem it solves → how it solves it (input → process → output).
technology.exampleHook: Use something the child already uses: WhatsApp message, Google search, GPS.

environment.name: Environment / Earth Science
environment.analogyDomain: Our shared home — interconnected systems where every change has consequences
environment.howItWorks: Human action → change in natural system → consequence for living things.
environment.exampleHook: A local Indian example first (Ganga, Himalayan glaciers, Indian forests).

gk.name: General Knowledge
gk.analogyDomain: Facts with memory hooks — comparisons, surprises, connections to daily life
gk.howItWorks: State the fact → give ONE memorable hook (comparison, surprise, or connection).
gk.exampleHook: Pacific Ocean = all land on Earth fits inside it. Sun = 8 minutes for light to reach us.

civics.name: Civics / Social Studies
civics.analogyDomain: School rules → city rules → country laws. Fairness, rights, shared decisions.
civics.howItWorks: Connect to child's life first, then zoom out to government. Three-job framework (make/run/check laws).
civics.exampleHook: Right to Education Act: before it, millions couldn't go to school. After it, every child can.

health.name: Health & Nutrition
health.analogyDomain: The child's own body right now — fuel, building blocks, protection tools
health.howItWorks: What goes in → what the body does with it → what the result is. Always connect to feelings.
health.exampleHook: Things the child has felt: hunger, tiredness after exercise, a cut healing, feeling full.

sports.name: Sports
sports.analogyDomain: Rules as solutions to problems — strategy, technique, and the science behind performance
sports.howItWorks: Why the rule exists + what would happen without it + how it shapes the game.
sports.exampleHook: India-first: cricket, kabaddi, hockey, badminton. Use a famous real moment.

general.name: General
general.analogyDomain: Games, sports, family life — how everyday things work
general.howItWorks: Everyday actions — how things work in daily life
general.exampleHook: Use something from the child's daily experience.
